summary: Gruber challenges the popular interpretation of Apple's amended complaint against Psystar, which included unnamed 'John Doe' defendants. While most commentators assumed Apple was alleging a shadowy corporate backer behind Psystar, Gruber proposes a simpler explanation: the John Does are likely contributors to the OSx86 Project, the open-source effort that makes running Mac OS X on non-Apple hardware possible. He argues Psystar is exactly what it appears to be — a brazen fly-by-night operation — and that Apple's real strategic interest lies in shutting down the OSx86 Project rather than just Psystar itself. He notes that eliminating Psystar alone wouldn't prevent future cloners from using the same freely available tools.
key_points: The 'John Doe defendants' in Apple's complaint are more likely OSx86 Project contributors than secret corporate backers of Psystar
Psystar's clone operation requires nothing more than commodity hardware and the OSx86 Project's work — there's no need for a conspiracy theory
Shutting down Psystar alone wouldn't stop Mac cloning; Apple's strategic interest is in eliminating the underlying OSx86 tooling
Hiring good lawyers doesn't indicate deep-pocketed backers — it just means Psystar had the sense to hire good lawyers
money_quotes: All it takes to do what they've done is a pair of brass balls.
The idea that they would ever get away with it is stupid, so it doesn't make much sense to think that there are any well-established companies backing Psystar's endeavor.
If it looks like a cockamamie scheme from a fly-by-night operation, it probably is.
Hiring good lawyers indicates nothing more than that Psystar had the good sense to hire good lawyers.
Psystar's secret sauce — getting Mac OS X to run on non-Apple computers — isn't theirs, but rather comes from the OSx86 Project.
Apple has more to gain by shutting down the OSx86 Project — or at least shutting down its major contributors — than by shutting down Psystar.
key_insight: Apple's unnamed 'John Doe' defendants likely target the OSx86 Project contributors who enable Mac cloning, not some shadowy corporate puppet master behind Psystar.
